turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#f7f7fb"/>

  <!-- subtle ground -->
  <path d="M0 420 C120 390, 210 440, 320 415 C420 392, 480 410, 512 405 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#e6f3ea"/>

  <!-- coins stack -->
  <g transform="translate(165,285)">
    <ellipse cx="92" cy="170" rx="115" ry="18" fill="#d9a441" opacity="0.35"/>

    <g>
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="140" rx="56" ry="16" fill="#f1c15a" stroke="#c58b2f" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="120" rx="56" ry="16" fill="#f4c86a" stroke="#c58b2f" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.98"/>
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="100" rx="56" ry="16" fill="#f1c15a" stroke="#c58b2f" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="80" rx="56" ry="16" fill="#f4c86a" stroke="#c58b2f" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.98"/>

      <!-- inner highlights -->
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="100" rx="20" ry="6" fill="#fff" opacity="0.35"/>
      <ellipse cx="92" cy="120" rx="20" ry="6" fill="#fff" opacity="0.25"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- potted plant to suggest growth -->
  <g transform="translate(175,235)">
    <path d="M78 248 C105 240, 140 240, 166 248 L166 284 C140 296, 105 296, 78 284 Z" fill="#b56a3a" stroke="#8f4d2a" stroke-width="4"/>
    <path d="M66 248 L178 248" stroke="#8f4d2a" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M96 248 C98 210, 146 210, 150 248" fill="#c97a43" stroke="#8f4d2a" stroke-width="4"/>

    <!-- leaves -->
    <path d="M115 220 C95 205, 90 180, 112 170 C132 160, 150 175, 145 198 C141 217, 126 225, 115 220 Z" fill="#38a169" stroke="#1f6f3f" stroke-width="4"/>
    <path d="M145 220 C160 202, 184 192, 200 205 C216 218, 206 242, 184 248 C165 254, 150 240, 145 220 Z" fill="#34d399" stroke="#1f6f3f" stroke-width="4"/>
    <path d="M128 236 C126 210, 132 190, 140 170" fill="none" stroke="#1f6f3f" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- upward arrow / growth indicator -->
  <g transform="translate(60,70)" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
    <!-- axis -->
    <path d="M220 360 C250 320, 270 290, 300 250" fill="none" stroke="#1f9d55" stroke-width="10" opacity="0.12"/>

    <!-- arrow shaft -->
    <path d="M250 365 C285 320, 306 290, 346 235" fill="none" stroke="#1f9d55" stroke-width="10"/>

    <!-- arrow head -->
    <path d="M350 235 L372 215 L365 246 Z" fill="#1f9d55" stroke="#0b5c2b" stroke-width="4"/>

    <!-- accent dots / small bars -->
    <circle cx="250" cy="365" r="8" fill="#0ea5e9" opacity="0.9"/>
    <circle cx="300" cy="305" r="8" fill="#0ea5e9" opacity="0.8"/>
    <circle cx="338" cy="260" r="8" fill="#0ea5e9" opacity="0.7"/>
  </g>

  <!-- light background chart line -->
  <path d="M70 380 C125 340, 170 370, 210 330 C250 292, 295 310, 340 265 C385 220, 425 235, 462 210" fill="none" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.22"/>
  <path d="M70 380 C125 340, 170 370, 210 330 C250 292, 295 310, 340 265 C385 220, 425 235, 462 210" fill="none" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.35"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating an investment illustration**

I need to design a visual representation of "investment" without using any text. I’m thinking about using a coin with an upward arrow or a piggy bank with a growth chart. I want to create it with SVG shapes like stacked coins and a rising arrow. A small plant in a coin pot might work too, with the background light. I’ll keep it simple, using paths and circles to convey the idea effectively!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

message.reasoning:
**Exploring investment concepts**
